title: spermacoce spicata
intro: Spermacoce spicata, also known as the buttonweed, is a perennial herbaceous plant belonging to the Rubiaceae family. The plant has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antiseptic properties. The health benefits associated with Spermacoce spicata include relief from fever, rheumatism, and skin conditions, as well as its ability to aid in the treatment of wounds and ulcers. Spermacoce spicata is commonly used in herbal preparations such as teas, infusions, and tinctures, and is also used in folk medicine to treat a range of ailments.
